label: PASS
rationale: Locking_Range1 is preconfigured with LockOnReset Power Cycle, i.e. reset_types value 0, and the authenticated Admins Get ACL includes LockOnReset. Returning column 9 as [0] is consistent with the preconfigured value and readable Get behavior.

label: FAIL
rationale: There is no prior Locking Set changing LockOnReset, so Locking_Range1 should still report its preconfigured Power Cycle value [0]. Returning [1] instead does not match the Opal preconfiguration and reset_types value mapping.

label: PASS
rationale: A successful Set wrote LockOnReset to [0,3], which Opal requires support for. Core requires all Set changes to be made on success, and an authorized Get of the readable LockOnReset cell then returns [0,3].

label: FAIL
rationale: The prior Locking Set to LockOnReset [0,3] returned SUCCESS, so Core requires that change to have been made. A later authorized Get of column 9 returning only [0] fails to reflect the successful Set value.

label: PASS
rationale: Core defines an empty LockOnReset set as valid semantics for no ReadLocked/WriteLocked change on reset. Given the prior Set of column 9 to [] returned SUCCESS, Core requires the change to be made, and the authorized Get returns the same empty set.

label: FAIL
rationale: The previous Set of LockOnReset to [] returned SUCCESS, so the Set changes should have taken effect. Returning [0] on the subsequent authorized Get does not reflect the last successful value.

label: PASS
rationale: Opal permits a TPer to support LockOnReset [0,1], and the Set to that value returned SUCCESS. Since a successful Set must apply all submitted changes, the later authorized Get correctly reports [0,1].

label: FAIL
rationale: The prior Set to LockOnReset [0,1] returned SUCCESS; because successful Set applies all changes, the subsequent authorized Get should return [0,1]. Returning only [0] loses the Hardware reset type and is not compliant.
